sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board changed after pressing RIGHT! Let me compare what changed. Looking at the top bar:
- Before: the right end had blue (b) then sky blue (S)
- After: it seems like the sky blue (S) and blue (b) swapped positions
Let me look more carefully at the top region to see what changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before/after of RIGHT action
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = last_transition.after_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Find changed rows
changes = []
for i in range(64):
    if before[i] != after[i]:
        changes.append(i)
        
print(f"Changed rows: {changes}")
for r in changes:
    print(f"Row {r}:")
    print(f"  before: {before[r]}")
    print(f"  after:  {after[r]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Now I can see the change. On the right side of the horizontal bar, the pattern `bbSS` changed to `SSbb`. The blue (b) and sky blue (S) segments swapped positions. This means RIGHT moves the blue segment to the right (or the sky blue segment to the left).
So the top bar has some kind of sliding puzzle mechanic. The blue segments can move left/right. Let me explore more to understand the full mechanics. Let me try pressing RIGHT again to see if it continues to shift.
